description: The completed-harness false-positive fix is tracked as its own harness task so future sessions do not confuse it with the earlier active-harness conflict work.
steps: Open PLAN.json and verify it only tracks the completed-harness false-positive fix.
Open PROGRESS.md and verify the reboot entry explains that completed harnesses were being mislabeled as unfinished.
Confirm the scope is limited to harness-state detection, conflict wording, and prompt semantics.

description: Repo harness detection distinguishes missing, active, and completed harness states based on PLAN.json completion instead of file presence alone.
steps: Inspect a repo with all harness files present and unfinished PLAN.json items.
Verify detect_repo_harness reports active for unfinished plans and completed for plans with zero remaining work.
Confirm missing and partial harness repos still report missing or initializing correctly.

description: Telegram start-coding flow pauses for confirmation on both active and completed harness repos, but uses different conflict reasons and user-facing wording.
steps: Send a private-chat start command for an unfinished harness repo and verify nanobot says the repo has an unfinished harness.
Send the same flow for a completed harness repo and verify nanobot says the repo has a completed harness that can be used as historical context.
Confirm repos without a complete harness still auto-start directly.

description: Codex bootstrap prompt semantics reflect the difference between completed and unfinished harnesses, so completed plans are treated as background context rather than pending work.
steps: Inspect the prompt generated for an unfinished harness repo and verify it still restores the current unfinished task state first.
Inspect the prompt generated for a completed harness repo and verify it describes the old harness as completed historical context.
Confirm the start_new_goal override still tells Codex not to resume unfinished work as the primary task.

description: Focused tests cover completed-harness detection, waiting-user reporting, router branching, notifier compatibility, and prompt generation.
steps: Run focused pytest coverage for harness, reporting, router, worker, and notifier flows.
Verify the suite covers both repo_active_harness and repo_completed_harness conflict reasons.
Confirm compileall still succeeds for the touched coding-task modules and tests.

description: A real codex-remote validation path shows the completed harness now triggers completed-context wording instead of the old unfinished-harness false positive.
steps: Use the real /Users/miau/Documents/codex-remote repo, whose current PLAN.json is fully complete.
Simulate a Telegram start-coding request against that repo through the live routing stack.
Verify the response explicitly says the repo has a completed harness that can be used as historical context.
